claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your situation. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Her name is Alex and she's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking some key questions. First:

**Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you live with her and are you responsible for paying for things like her food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all her needs - I pay for her food, clothes, school things, everything she needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Where do you live? Are you living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Let me now ask:

**Is Alex under 16 years old, or if she's 16 or over, is she in approved education or training?**

(I know you mentioned she's 8, but I need to confirm for the eligibility assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is 8 years old, so she's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Does Alex live with anyone else who might also be responsible for her, such as another parent or partner?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she just lives with me. It's just the two of us at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask one more important question:

**Is Alex currently in any of these situations:**
- Living in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks (or expected to)?
- Living with someone else (not you) for some or all of the time?
- In custody (detained)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Alex lives at home with me full-time and isn't in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Before I make my final assessment, let me just read the official eligibility guidance to ensure I give you the correct determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on all the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now make my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live with her in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for her and pay for all her needs ✓
- There are no exceptional circumstances affecting eligibility ✓

You meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for your daughter.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit and either receive the payments or opt out of receiving payments but still get National Insurance credits.

If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by contacting the Child Benefit Office.

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) She is under 16 years old (age 8), which satisfies the age requirement; (2) You are responsible for her as you live with her and pay for all her needs including food, clothes and other essentials; (3) You live in the UK; (4) Alex lives with you full-time and is not in any of the exceptional circumstances that would affect eligibility (not in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks, not living with someone else, not in custody). According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, they pay for the child's needs, and there are no exceptional circumstances (such as the child being in hospital/care or living elsewhere) that would affect eligibility.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) She is under 16 years old (age 8), which satisfies the age requirement; (2) You are responsible for her as you live with her and pay for all her needs including food, clothes and other essentials; (3) You live in the UK; (4) Alex lives with you full-time and is not in any of the exceptional circumstances that would affect eligibility (not in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks, not living with someone else, not in custody). According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, they pay for the child's needs, and there are no exceptional circumstances (such as the child being in hospital/care or living elsewhere) that would affect eligibility.
